wikitext:
{{Chess Opening Theory/Position |Benko gambit |parent=[[../|Benko gambit]] |eco=[[Chess/ECOA|A57]] |responses=[[/4...a6|4...a6]] }} == 4. cxb5 == White accepts the initial pawn. The "real" gambit now follows with [[/4...a6|'''4...a6''']], where White can take another pawn but Black can only recover one. In exchange for being a pawn down, Black hopes to capitalise on the open a- and b-files and a good structure in the form of a single large pawn island. 4...a6 is essentially the only move. Note that '''4...Qa5+?''' does not recover the pawn as 5. Nc3 interposes check and defends b5. == Theory table == {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 c5 3. d5 b5 4. cxb5'''
4 5 6 7
...
[[/4...a6|a6]]		 bxa6
Bxa6		 Nc3
d6		 Nf3
g6
